mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in over 60% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative mean 60-day forward return, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 20-day forward returns during early-cycle expansions, or the out-of-sample hit rate drops below 50% in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP confirms macroeconomic expansion and supports corporate profit forecasts, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases over a 10-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 65% of periods where CPI inflation exceeds the 3.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates robust job creation and labor market tightening, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns, yielding a mean forward return below zero during mid-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales volumes signal weakening consumer demand and impending corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations during late-cycle contraction regimes.
